meta.title: AI Exposure of the Canadian Job Market
meta.primary_rollup_note: The dashboard rolls canonical NOC 2021 unit groups through the 45 official major groups.
meta.metric_model_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables are published at a coarser occupation grain than official NOC 2021 unit groups, so the unit-group labour metrics are allocated using ESDC unit employment weights. Outlook remains direct from the ESDC unit-group release.
meta.exposure_metric_scale: 0-10 display score derived from the share of workers in high-exposure EPIAC occupations
meta.exposure_note: Official EPIAC fields are mapped from published StatCan occupation groups onto the canonical NOC 2021 unit groups.
meta.profile_layer_note: Official OaSIS occupational profiles attach to canonical NOC 2021 unit groups through an explicit generated mapping table.
meta.source: Statistics Canada annual occupation tables rebuilt onto the canonical NOC 2021 spine

source_tables.name: 14-10-0416-01 Labour force characteristics by occupation, annual
Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024
Occupational and Skills Information System (OaSIS) - 2025 Version 1.0

oasis.title: Occupational and Skills Information System (OaSIS) - 2025 Version 1.0

family.title: Labourers in processing, manufacturing and utilities
family.definition: This major group comprises labourers in processing, manufacturing and utilities. These occupations come with Training, Education, Experience and Responsibility (TEER) identified as TEER 5. Occupations classified in TEER 5, as those included in this group with a 5 as the second digit of their code, are typically accessible with short work demonstration and no formal educational requirements.
family.major_group_title: Labourers in processing, manufacturing and utilities
family.exposure_rationale: Aggregated from canonical unit groups. EPIAC values roll up unit-level mappings from published StatCan occupation groups.
family.metric_rationale: This major group aggregates canonical unit groups. Labour-market counts and wages reflect the unit-group estimates allocated from published StatCan annual occupation tables using ESDC employment weights.
family.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024
epiac_source_groups: Assemblers and labourers in manufacturing and utilities (95, 96)

units.title: Labourers in mineral and metal processing
units.major_group_title: Labourers in processing, manufacturing and utilities
units.sub_major_group_title: Labourers in processing, manufacturing and utilities
units.minor_group_title: Labourers in processing, manufacturing and utilities
units.definition: Labourers in mineral and metal processing perform material handling, clean-up, packaging and other elemental activities related to mineral ore and metal processing. They are employed in mineral ore and metal processing plants such as copper, lead and zinc refineries, uranium processing plants, steel mills, aluminum plants, precious metal refineries, cement processing plants, clay, glass and stone processing plants and foundries.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.metric_rationale: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.exposure_rationale: Mapped to the closest published StatCan EPIAC occupation group from the 2021 Census (NOC 2016): Assemblers and labourers in manufacturing and utilities (95, 96).
units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024
epiac_source_groups: Assemblers and labourers in manufacturing and utilities (95, 96)

core_competencies.statement: to handle raw materials, intermediate products and finished goods throughout processing operations
to operate various types of equipment and machinery for tasks such as material transportation, sorting, cleaning or packaging
to perform tasks and sustain energy levels throughout the day, such as when feeding conveyors, shovelling cement, or transporting stamping materials
to maneuver effectively and safely heavy pieces of metal or mineral
to ensure the smooth flow of operations, especially when assisting process and machine operators

core_competencies.competency: Knowledge in Manufacturing, Processing and Production
core_competencies.statement: to be familiar with the types of minerals and metals processed and the different stages of processing such as crushing, grinding, flotation or smelting

units.title: Labourers in metal fabrication
units.major_group_title: Labourers in processing, manufacturing and utilities
units.sub_major_group_title: Labourers in processing, manufacturing and utilities
units.minor_group_title: Labourers in processing, manufacturing and utilities
units.definition: Labourers in metal fabrication remove excess metal and unwanted materials from metal parts, castings and other metal products and perform other labouring activities. They are employed in structural steel, boiler and platework fabrication plants, heavy machinery manufacturing plants, sheet metal fabrication shops, shipbuilding and other metal products manufacturing companies.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.metric_rationale: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Labourers in metal fabrication remove excess metal and unwanted materials from metal parts, castings and other metal products and perform other labouring activities.
employment_requirements: Some secondary school education may be required.
additional_information: There is some mobility among occupations in this unit group.
Progression to metalworking machine operator positions is possible with experience.
workplaces_employers: Structural steel, boiler and platework fabrication plants

core_competencies.statement: to achieve desired outcomes, such as accurate measurements, smooth finishes and proper assembly of metal parts
to ensure fabricated metal parts meet specifications and quality standards through visual inspections, measurements, or simple tests
to perform repetitive tasks, such as grinding, cutting, or polishing metal parts, for extended shifts
to safely lift, carry and maneuver heavy objects, such as metal parts, hand carts or dollies
to work alongside platework fitters, metalworking machine operators and other personnel in ensuring the efficient operation of facilities

core_competencies.competency: Knowledge in Manufacturing, Processing and Production
core_competencies.statement: to understand metal fabrication techniques and processes, enabling workers to effectively follow production schedules and maintain adherence to quality standards

units.title: Labourers in chemical products processing and utilities
units.major_group_title: Labourers in processing, manufacturing and utilities
units.sub_major_group_title: Labourers in processing, manufacturing and utilities
units.minor_group_title: Labourers in processing, manufacturing and utilities
units.definition: Labourers in chemical products processing and utilities carry out a variety of material handling, cleaning and routine general labouring activities. They are employed by petroleum and natural gas processing, pipeline and petrochemical, chemical and pharmaceutical companies, and by electrical, water and waste treatment utilities and services.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

oasis_profiles.profile_title: Labourers in chemical products processing and utilities
oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Labourers in chemical products processing and utilities
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Labourers in chemical products processing and utilities carry out a variety of material handling, cleaning and routine general labouring activities.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school may be required.
additional_information: Considerable mobility is possible among jobs in this unit group.
Progression to operator positions in the chemical or utility industry is possible with experience and appropriate qualifications.
workplaces_employers: Electrical, water and waste treatment utilities and services
Petroleum and natural gas processing companies

core_competencies.statement: to minimize waste and maintain safety by following proper storage or handling of materials
to ensure efficient chemical processing operations by maneuvering equipment such as pumps and valves
to perform tasks, such as cleaning, material handling and routine maintenance, for extended periods, often in challenging environments
to safely perform physical tasks such as handling drums of chemicals, transporting equipment, or assisting in the installation of machinery
to work alongside chemical machine operators, technicians and other personnel in ensuring the efficient operation of facilities

core_competencies.competency: Knowledge in Manufacturing, Processing and Production
core_competencies.statement: to understand processing techniques required to maintain product quality and contribute to process improvements

units.title: Labourers in wood, pulp and paper processing
units.major_group_title: Labourers in processing, manufacturing and utilities
units.sub_major_group_title: Labourers in processing, manufacturing and utilities
units.minor_group_title: Labourers in processing, manufacturing and utilities
units.definition: Labourers in wood, pulp and paper processing carry out a variety of general labouring and routine wood processing activities and assist pulp mill and papermaking machine operators. They are employed by pulp and paper, and paper converting companies, sawmills, planing mills, wood treatment plants, waferboard plants and other wood processing companies.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. oasis_profiles.profile_title: Labourers in wood, pulp and paper processing
oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Labourers in wood, pulp and paper processing
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Labourers in wood, pulp and paper processing carry out a variety of general labouring and routine wood processing activities and assist pulp mill and papermaking machine operators.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is required by the pulp and paper industry and by other large employers.
additional_information: Progression to machine or plant operating positions is possible with experience.
There is considerable mobility among jobs within individual establishments.

example_titles.job_title_text: beater operator helper - pulp and paper

core_competencies.statement: to handle tools such as saws, grinders or conveyors in order to process wood and prepare it for further production stages

core_competencies.competency: Operation Monitoring of Machinery and Equipment
core_competencies.statement: to detect abnormalities or issues that may affect production quality or safety, which involves observing indicators, gauges and computer screens
to perform tasks, such as feeding logs onto conveyors or aligning logs manually, for extended periods
to safely handle heavy materials such as logs, lumber and equipment
to assist other wood processing workers in operating, maintaining and repairing various machines and equipment

core_competencies.competency: Knowledge in Manufacturing, Processing and Production
core_competencies.statement: to understand how raw materials are transformed into finished products helps them perform tasks more effectively and in alignment with the broader production goals

units.title: Labourers in textile processing and cutting
units.major_group_title: Labourers in processing, manufacturing and utilities
units.sub_major_group_title: Labourers in processing, manufacturing and utilities
units.minor_group_title: Labourers in processing, manufacturing and utilities
units.definition: Labourers in textile processing perform a variety of manual duties to assist in processing fibres into yarn or thread, or to assist in weaving, knitting, bleaching, dyeing or finishing textile fabrics or other textile products. Labourers in textile cutting cut fabric, fur, or leather to make parts for garments, linens, shoes and other articles. Labourers in textile processing and cutting are employed by clothing and textile manufacturers and other manufacturers of fabric products such as leather and fur.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Labourers in textile processing and cutting
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Labourers in textile processing perform a variety of manual duties to assist in processing fibres into yarn or thread, or to assist in weaving, knitting, bleaching, dyeing or finishing textile fabrics or other textile products.
main_duties: Clean textile machines and work areas
Push carts, trucks, or cans of fibres from one work area to another
Assist machine operators and other textile workers as required.
employment_requirements: Some secondary school education may be required.
On-the-job training is provided for some occupations in this unit group.
additional_information: Progression to machine operator or supervisory positions is possible with experience.
There is little mobility among the different kinds of cutters in this unit group.

oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Labourers in textile processing and cutting
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Fabric cutters cut fabric to make parts for garments, linens and other articles.
main_duties: Place patterns on top of layers of fabric and cut fabric using electric or manual knives, cutters or computer numerically controlled (CNC) cutting devices
Adjust cutting technique to type of fabric and style of garment
employment_requirements: Some secondary school education may be required.
Experience as a fabric, fur or leather cutter is required for fabric, fur or leather sample cutters.
On-the-job training is provided for some occupations in this unit group.
additional_information: Progression to machine operator or supervisory positions is possible with experience.
There is little mobility among the different kinds of cutters in this unit group.

example_titles.job_title_text: hand cutter - fabric, fur and leather products manufacturing

oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Labourers in textile processing and cutting
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Fur cutters cut fur pelts to make parts for garments and other fur articles.
main_duties: Cut fur skins in diagonal strips to specified length and shape of pelt using furrier's knives and cutters
Number pelts to indicate their location on pattern
May sort or match skins, and may prepare fur samples.
employment_requirements: Some secondary school education may be required.
Experience as a fabric, fur or leather cutter is required for fabric, fur or leather sample cutters.
On-the-job training is provided for some occupations in this unit group.
additional_information: Progression to machine operator or supervisory positions is possible with experience.
There is little mobility among the different kinds of cutters in this unit group.

oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Labourers in textile processing and cutting
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Leather cutters cut leather to make parts for shoes, garments and other leather articles.
main_duties: Select leather and cutting dies for leather goods and shoe parts according to specifications
Position leather on cutting bed of machine, maximizing usage according to skin grain, skin flaws and skin stretch
Operate clicker or punch press to stretch leather
Operate machine to strip and cut leather or cut single leather skins by hand to make leather products
employment_requirements: Some secondary school education may be required.
Experience as a fabric, fur or leather cutter is required for fabric, fur or leather sample cutters.
On-the-job training is provided for some occupations in this unit group.
additional_information: Progression to machine operator or supervisory positions is possible with experience.
There is little mobility among the different kinds of cutters in this unit group.
workplaces_employers: Leather garment and other products manufacturers

units.major_group_title: Labourers in processing, manufacturing and utilities
units.sub_major_group_title: Labourers in processing, manufacturing and utilities
units.minor_group_title: Labourers in processing, manufacturing and utilities
units.definition: Labourers in food and beverage processing perform material handling, clean-up, packaging and other elemental activities related to food and beverage processing. They are employed in fruit and vegetable processing plants, dairies, flour mills, bakeries, sugar refineries, meat plants, breweries and other food and beverage processing and packaging plants.

core_competencies.statement: to handle, maintain inventory control of and properly store ingredients, packaging materials and finished products
to operate various equipment and machinery involved in food and beverage processing, such as mixers, conveyors and packaging machines
to perform routine tasks such as material handling, machine operation and cleaning for extended periods, often in fast-paced environments
to handle heavy materials when loading ingredients into machinery, transporting packaging materials, or assisting with equipment setup
to coordinate workflow and maintain productivity while assisting food and beverage machine operators, technicians and other team members

core_competencies.competency: Knowledge in Manufacturing, Processing and Production
core_competencies.statement: to understand food safety standards, hygiene practices and quality control measures

units.title: Labourers in fish and seafood processing
units.major_group_title: Labourers in processing, manufacturing and utilities
units.sub_major_group_title: Labourers in processing, manufacturing and utilities
units.minor_group_title: Labourers in processing, manufacturing and utilities
units.definition: Labourers in fish and seafood processing perform clean-up, packaging, material handling and other elemental activities related to fish and seafood processing. They are employed in fish and seafood processing and packaging plants.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.metric_rationale: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.exposure_rationale: Mapped to the closest published StatCan EPIAC occupation group from the 2021 Census (NOC 2016): Assemblers and labourers in manufacturing and utilities (95, 96).
units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024
epiac_source_groups: Assemblers and labourers in manufacturing and utilities (95, 96)

oasis_profiles.profile_title: Labourers in fish and seafood processing
oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Labourers in fish and seafood processing
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Labourers in fish and seafood processing perform clean-up, packaging, material handling and other elemental activities related to fish and seafood processing.
employment_requirements: Some secondary school education may be required.
additional_information: Progression to other occupations in the fish processing industry is possible with experience.
workplaces_employers: Fish and seafood processing and packaging plants

core_competencies.statement: to handle materials including raw fish, packaging materials, cleaning supplies or equipment, in such a way that minimizes waste and optimizes efficiency
to maintain the pace and quality of production by effectively controlling machinery or equipment, such as filleting machines, packaging machines and cleaning equipment
to consistently perform physically demanding tasks such as unloading fish from fishing vessels or loading fish onto processing equipment
to handle heavy materials such as crates of fish, equipment and packaging materials
to ensure smooth workflow and enhance productivity by assisting machine operators, quality control personnel and other members of the team of the fish processing plants

core_competencies.competency: Knowledge in Manufacturing, Processing and Production
core_competencies.statement: to follow standardized procedures and ensure product safety and quality, which requires an understanding of principles such as hygiene, sanitation standards or processing techniques

units.title: Other labourers in processing, manufacturing and utilities
units.major_group_title: Labourers in processing, manufacturing and utilities
units.sub_major_group_title: Labourers in processing, manufacturing and utilities
units.minor_group_title: Labourers in processing, manufacturing and utilities
units.definition: Other labourers in processing, manufacturing and utilities perform material handling, clean-up, packaging and other elemental activities in processing, manufacturing and utilities. They are employed by companies that manufacture products such as clothing, footwear, furniture and electrical and electronic products and by printing and packaging companies.

Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

additional_information: There is some mobility among occupations in this unit group.
Progression to machine operating positions is possible with experience.

core_competencies.statement: to handle material resources such as raw materials, components, tools and finished products to ensure minimal wastage
to start and stop equipment or adjust settings as required to ensure tasks are performed efficiently and accurately
to perform physically demanding work that usually requires endurance and sustained effort
to handle heavy materials, equipment and products during daily tasks
to work with others during tasks that require teamwork, such as assisting operators, assemblers or other workers

core_competencies.competency: Knowledge in Manufacturing, Processing and Production
core_competencies.statement: to understand the production goals of the company and anticipate workflow needs
